title: How did exporting firms cope with the crisis? — EFIGE Country Report: Hungary
summary_en: Hungary-specific country report from the EFIGE project. Documents how Hungarian exporting firms coped with the 2008–9 crisis: exporters did not exit at higher rates than non-exporters, but their export volumes contracted sharply, especially for single-product firms. Trade-credit constraints and demand shocks dominate the explanation; productivity differences alone cannot account for the patterns.
policy_relevance: Crisis-time policy lessons for the Hungarian government and EU institutions: trade finance and export-credit insurance matter more than corporate income tax or labour-market policy in stabilising the export base. Supports the case for the Eximbank and EU-level crisis trade-credit facilities. Caveat: the report is descriptive; causal identification of the channels would need follow-up work.
